title: Implement 4-stage data pipeline from tests (linear dependency chain)
goal: Complete the implementation in stage1_parser.py, stage2_validator.py, stage3_transformer.py, and stage4_aggregator.py to pass all tests. Tests define expected behavior.
hints: Files depend linearly: stage1 <- stage2 <- stage3 <- stage4. Implement in order. Read all tests first.

seed_files.tests.py:
import pytest
from stage1_parser import Record, ParseError, parse_line, parse_batch
from stage2_validator import (
    ValidationRule, Validator, required_field, numeric_field, min_value
)
from stage3_transformer import (
    Transform, TransformPipeline, rename_field, add_field, compute_field
)
from stage4_aggregator import Aggregator


# ===== Stage 1: Parser tests =====

class TestParser:

    def test_parse_line_basic(self):
        rec = parse_line("name=Alice;age=30")
        assert rec.get("name") == "Alice"
        assert rec.get("age") == "30"
        assert rec.errors == []

    def test_parse_line_whitespace(self):
        rec = parse_line("  name = Alice ; age = 30  ")
        assert rec.get("name") == "Alice"
        assert rec.get("age") == "30"

    def test_parse_line_invalid_pair(self):
        rec = parse_line("name=Alice;badpair;age=30")
        assert rec.get("name") == "Alice"
        assert rec.get("age") == "30"
        assert len(rec.errors) == 1
        assert "invalid pair: badpair" in rec.errors[0]

    def test_parse_line_empty_value(self):
        rec = parse_line("name=;age=30")
        assert rec.get("name") == ""
        assert rec.get("age") == "30"

    def test_parse_batch_multiline(self):
        text = "name=Alice;age=30\nname=Bob;age=25"
        records = parse_batch(text)
        assert len(records) == 2
        assert records[0].get("name") == "Alice"
        assert records[1].get("name") == "Bob"

    def test_parse_batch_skips_empty_lines(self):
        text = "name=Alice\n\n\nname=Bob\n"
        records = parse_batch(text)
        assert len(records) == 2


# ===== Stage 2: Validator tests =====

class TestValidator:

    def test_required_field_pass(self):
        rule = required_field("name")
        rec = Record({"name": "Alice"})
        assert rule.validate(rec) == ""

    def test_required_field_fail_missing(self):
        rule = required_field("name")
        rec = Record({})
        result = rule.validate(rec)
        assert result != ""

    def test_required_field_fail_empty(self):
        rule = required_field("name")
        rec = Record({"name": ""})
        result = rule.validate(rec)
        assert result != ""

    def test_numeric_field_pass(self):
        rule = numeric_field("age")
        rec = Record({"age": "30"})
        assert rule.validate(rec) == ""

    def test_numeric_field_pass_float(self):
        rule = numeric_field("price")
        rec = Record({"price": "19.99"})
        assert rule.validate(rec) == ""

    def test_numeric_field_fail(self):
        rule = numeric_field("age")
        rec = Record({"age": "abc"})
        result = rule.validate(rec)
        assert result != ""

    def test_numeric_field_missing_is_valid(self):
        rule = numeric_field("age")
        rec = Record({})
        assert rule.validate(rec) == ""

    def test_min_value_pass(self):
        rule = min_value("age", 18)
        rec = Record({"age": "25"})
        assert rule.validate(rec) == ""

    def test_min_value_fail(self):
        rule = min_value("age", 18)
        rec = Record({"age": "10"})
        result = rule.validate(rec)
        assert result != ""

    def test_min_value_missing_is_valid(self):
        rule = min_value("age", 18)
        rec = Record({})
        assert rule.validate(rec) == ""

    def test_min_value_non_numeric_is_valid(self):
        rule = min_value("age", 18)
        rec = Record({"age": "abc"})
        assert rule.validate(rec) == ""

    def test_validate_collects_all_errors(self):
        v = Validator()
        v.add_rule(required_field("name"))
        v.add_rule(required_field("email"))
        rec = Record({})
        errors = v.validate(rec)
        assert len(errors) == 2
        assert len(rec.errors) == 2

    def test_validate_batch_splits(self):
        v = Validator()
        v.add_rule(required_field("name"))
        records = [
            Record({"name": "Alice"}),
            Record({}),
            Record({"name": "Charlie"}),
        ]
        valid, invalid = v.validate_batch(records)
        assert len(valid) == 2
        assert len(invalid) == 1

    def test_validate_batch_includes_parse_errors(self):
        v = Validator()
        v.add_rule(required_field("name"))
        rec_with_parse_error = Record({"name": "Alice"})
        rec_with_parse_error.errors.append("parse error")
        records = [rec_with_parse_error, Record({"name": "Bob"})]
        valid, invalid = v.validate_batch(records)
        assert len(valid) == 1
        assert len(invalid) == 1
        assert invalid[0] is rec_with_parse_error


# ===== Stage 3: Transformer tests =====

class TestTransformer:

    def test_rename_field_existing(self):
        t = rename_field("name", "full_name")
        rec = Record({"name": "Alice", "age": "30"}, raw="name=Alice;age=30")
        result = t.apply(rec)
        assert result.get("full_name") == "Alice"
        assert result.has("name") is False
        assert result.get("age") == "30"

    def test_rename_field_missing(self):
        t = rename_field("name", "full_name")
        rec = Record({"age": "30"}, raw="age=30")
        result = t.apply(rec)
        assert result.has("full_name") is False
        assert result.get("age") == "30"

    def test_add_field(self):
        t = add_field("status", "active")
        rec = Record({"name": "Alice"}, raw="name=Alice")
        result = t.apply(rec)
        assert result.get("status") == "active"
        assert result.get("name") == "Alice"

    def test_compute_field(self):
        t = compute_field("greeting", lambda fields: f"Hello {fields.get('name', 'stranger')}")
        rec = Record({"name": "Alice"}, raw="name=Alice")
        result = t.apply(rec)
        assert result.get("greeting") == "Hello Alice"

    def test_pipeline_applies_in_order(self):
        pipe = TransformPipeline()
        pipe.add(rename_field("name", "full_name"))
        pipe.add(add_field("version", "2"))
        rec = Record({"name": "Alice"}, raw="name=Alice")
        result = pipe.apply(rec)
        assert result.get("full_name") == "Alice"
        assert result.get("version") == "2"
        assert result.has("name") is False

    def test_pipeline_batch(self):
        pipe = TransformPipeline()
        pipe.add(add_field("processed", "yes"))
        records = [
            Record({"name": "Alice"}, raw="a"),
            Record({"name": "Bob"}, raw="b"),
        ]
        results = pipe.apply_batch(records)
        assert len(results) == 2
        assert all(r.get("processed") == "yes" for r in results)

    def test_transform_preserves_raw(self):
        t = add_field("extra", "val")
        rec = Record({"name": "Alice"}, raw="original raw line")
        result = t.apply(rec)
        assert result.raw == "original raw line"


# ===== Stage 4: Aggregator tests =====

class TestAggregator:

    def _make_records(self):
        return [
            Record({"dept": "eng", "name": "Alice", "salary": "100"}),
            Record({"dept": "eng", "name": "Bob", "salary": "120"}),
            Record({"dept": "sales", "name": "Charlie", "salary": "90"}),
            Record({"dept": "sales", "name": "Diana", "salary": "95"}),
            Record({"dept": "sales", "name": "Eve", "salary": "85"}),
        ]

    def test_group_basic(self):
        agg = Aggregator("dept")
        groups = agg.group(self._make_records())
        assert len(groups["eng"]) == 2
        assert len(groups["sales"]) == 3

    def test_group_unknown(self):
        agg = Aggregator("dept")
        records = [Record({"name": "Zach"})]
        groups = agg.group(records)
        assert len(groups["_unknown"]) == 1

    def test_count(self):
        agg = Aggregator("dept")
        counts = agg.count(self._make_records())
        assert counts["eng"] == 2
        assert counts["sales"] == 3

    def test_sum_field_basic(self):
        agg = Aggregator("dept")
        sums = agg.sum_field(self._make_records(), "salary")
        assert sums["eng"] == 220
        assert sums["sales"] == 270

    def test_sum_field_skips_non_numeric(self):
        agg = Aggregator("dept")
        records = [
            Record({"dept": "eng", "salary": "100"}),
            Record({"dept": "eng", "salary": "abc"}),
            Record({"dept": "eng", "salary": "50"}),
        ]
        sums = agg.sum_field(records, "salary")
        assert sums["eng"] == 150

    def test_avg_field_basic(self):
        agg = Aggregator("dept")
        avgs = agg.avg_field(self._make_records(), "salary")
        assert avgs["eng"] == 110
        assert avgs["sales"] == 90

    def test_avg_field_empty_group(self):
        agg = Aggregator("dept")
        records = [Record({"dept": "eng"})]
        avgs = agg.avg_field(records, "salary")
        assert avgs["eng"] == 0

    def test_summary_with_sums_and_avgs(self):
        agg = Aggregator("dept")
        result = agg.summary(self._make_records(), sum_fields=["salary"], avg_fields=["salary"])
        assert result["eng"]["count"] == 2
        assert result["eng"]["sums"]["salary"] == 220
        assert result["eng"]["avgs"]["salary"] == 110
        assert result["sales"]["count"] == 3
        assert result["sales"]["sums"]["salary"] == 270
        assert result["sales"]["avgs"]["salary"] == 90

    def test_summary_empty(self):
        agg = Aggregator("dept")
        result = agg.summary([], sum_fields=["salary"])
        assert result == {}

ground_truth: Implement all TODO stubs across 4 files in a linear dependency chain: (1) stage1_parser.py - parse_line splits on ';' then '=', strips whitespace, adds 'invalid pair' errors for pairs without '=', stores raw line; parse_batch splits on newlines skipping empty. (2) stage2_validator.py - ValidationRule.validate calls check_func returning '' or error string; Validator collects errors and appends to record.errors; validate_batch partitions into (valid, invalid) considering both parse and validation errors; required_field checks exists+non-empty; numeric_field tries float(); min_value checks float(val)>=minimum, skips missing/non-numeric. (3) stage3_transformer.py - Transform.apply calls func(fields) returning new Record preserving raw; TransformPipeline chains transforms; rename_field pops old key and sets new; add_field sets fixed value; compute_field calls func(fields). (4) stage4_aggregator.py - Aggregator.group uses group_by field with '_unknown' fallback; count/sum_field/avg_field operate per group; sum_field/avg_field skip non-numeric via float(); avg_field returns 0 for empty groups; summary combines count+sums+avgs per group.

eval_spec.check_script_content:
#!/bin/bash
set +e
cd "$WORKDIR"
result=$(python3 -m pytest tests.py -v --tb=short 2>&1)
echo "$result"
passed=$(echo "$result" | grep -c " PASSED")
failed=$(echo "$result" | grep -c " FAILED")
total=$((passed + failed))
echo ""
echo "Score: $passed/$total"
exit 0
